subcommands.name: -U
subcommands.description: Upgrade or add package(s) and install the required dependencies

name: -d
--nodeps
options.description: Skips dependency version checks

options.name: --assume-installed
options.description: Add a virtual package with version to the transaction to satisfy dependencies

options.name: --dbonly
options.description: Adds/removes the database entry only, leaving all files in place

options.name: --noprogressbar
options.description: Do not show a progress bar when downloading files

options.name: --noscriptlet
options.description: If an install scriptlet exists, do not execute it

name: -p
--print
options.description: Only print the targets instead of performing the actual operation

options.name: --print-format
options.description: Specify a printf-like format to control the output of the --print operation

name: -w
--downloadonly
options.description: Retrieve all packages from the server, but do not install/upgrade anything

options.name: --asdeps
options.description: Install packages non-explicitly by faking their install reason to be installed as a dependency

options.name: --asexplicit
options.description: Install packages explicitly by faking their install reason to be explicitly installed

options.name: --ignore
options.description: Directs pacman to ignore upgrades of a package even if there is one available

options.name: --ignoregroup
options.description: Directs pacman to ignore upgrades of all packages in a group even if there is one available

options.name: --needed
options.description: Do not reinstall the targets that are already up-to-date

options.name: --overwrite
options.description: Bypass file conflict checks and overwrite conflicting files

options.description: Mark a package as non-explicitly installed

options.name: --asexplicit
options.description: Mark a package as explicitly installed

name: -k
--check
options.description: Check the local package database is internally consistent

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Suppress messages on successful completions of database operations

options.name: --asdeps
options.description: Mark a package as non-explicitly installed

options.name: --asexplicit
options.description: Mark a package as explicitly installed

name: -k
--check
options.description: Check the local package database is internally consistent

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Suppress messages on successful completions of database operations

name: -c
--changelog
options.description: View the changelog of package if it exists

name: -d
--deps
options.description: Filter output to packages installed as dependencies

name: -e
--explicit
options.description: Filter output to explicitly installed packages

name: -g
--groups
options.description: Display all packages that are members of a named group

name: -i
--info
options.description: Display information on a given package

name: -k
--check
options.description: Check that all files owned by the given package(s) are present on the system

name: -l
--list
options.description: List all files owned by a given package

name: -m
--foreign
options.description: Filter output to packages that were not found in the sync database(s)

name: -n
--native
options.description: Filter output to packages that are found in the sync database(s)

name: -o
--owns
options.description: Search for the packages that own the specified file(s)

name: -p
--file
options.description: Signifies that the package supplied on the command line is a file and not an entry in the database

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Show less information for certain query operations

name: -s
--search
options.description: Search each locally-installed package for names or descriptions that match the given regex

name: -t
--unrequired
options.description: Filter output to print only packages neither required nor optionally required by any currently installed package

name: -u
--upgrades
options.description: Filter output to print only packages that are out-of-date on the local system

name: -c
--cascade
options.description: Remove all target packages, as well as all packages that depend on one or more target packages

name: -n
--nosave
options.description: Instructs pacman to ignore file backup designations

name: -s
--recursive
options.description: Remove each target specified including all of their dependencies, provided that they are not required by other packages and they were not explicity installed by the user

name: -u
-unneeded
options.description: Removes targets that are not required by any other packages

subcommands.name: -S
subcommands.description: Synchronize packages. Packages are installed directly from the remote repositories, including all dependencies

name: -d
--nodeps
options.description: Skips dependency version checks

options.name: --assume-installed
options.description: Add a virtual package with version to the transaction to satisfy dependencies

options.name: --dbonly
options.description: Adds/removes the database entry only, leaving all files in place

options.description: Do not show a progress bar when downloading files

options.description: If an install scriptlet exists, do not execute it

--print
options.description: Only print the targets instead of performing the actual operation

options.name: --print-format
options.description: Specify a printf-like format to control the output of the --print operation

name: -w
--downloadonly
options.description: Retrieve all packages from the server, but do not install/upgrade anything

options.name: --asdeps
options.description: Install packages non-explicitly by faking their install reason to be installed as a dependency

options.name: --asexplicit
options.description: Install packages explicitly by faking their install reason to be explicitly installed

options.name: --ignore
options.description: Directs pacman to ignore upgrades of a package even if there is one available

options.name: --ignoregroup
options.description: Directs pacman to ignore upgrades of all packages in a group even if there is one available

options.name: --needed
options.description: Do not reinstall the targets that are already up-to-date

options.name: --overwrite
options.description: Bypass file conflict checks and overwrite conflicting files

name: -c
--clean
options.description: Remvove packages that are no longer installed from the cache as well as currently unused sync databases

name: -g
--groups
options.description: Display all the members for each package group specified

name: -i
--info
options.description: Display information on a given sync database package

name: -l
--list
options.description: List all packages in the specified repositories

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Show less information for certain sync operations

name: -s
-search
options.description: This will search each package in the sync databases for names or descriptions that match the given regex

name: -u
--sysupgrade
options.description: Upgrades all packages that are out-of-date

name: -y
--refresh
options.description: Download a fresh copy of the master package database from the server(s)

name: -y
--refresh
options.description: Download fresh package databases from the server

--list
options.description: List the files owned by the queried package

name: -x
--regex
options.description: Interpret each query as a regular expression

--quiet
options.description: Show less information for certain file operations

options.description: Print each match in a machine readable output format

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display syntax for the given operation or if no operation was supplied, then the general syntax is shown

name: -V
--version
options.description: Display version and exit

name: -b
--dbpath
options.description: Specify an alternative database location

name: -r
--root
options.description: Specify an alternative installation root

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Output paths such as the Root, Conf File, DB Path, Cache Dirs, etc

options.name: --arch
options.description: Specify an alternate architecture

options.name: --cachedir
options.description: Specify an alternative package cache location

options.name: --color
options.description: Specify when to enable coloring

options.name: --config
options.description: Specify an alternate configuration file

options.name: --debug
options.description: Display debug messages

options.name: --gpgdir
options.description: Specify a directory of files used by GnuPG to verify package signatures

options.name: --logfile
options.description: Specify an alternate log file (absolute path)

options.name: --hookdir
options.description: Specify an alternative directory containing hook files

options.name: --noconfirm
options.description: Bypass any and all 'Are you sure?' messages

options.name: --confirm
options.description: Cancels the effects of a previous --noconfirm

options.name: --disable-download-timeout
options.description: Disable defaults for low speed limit and timeout on downloads

options.name: --sysroot
options.description: Specify an alternative system root
